title: Two Rooks, One Open File
setup: Two rooks on the **1st rank**, two pawns guarding the corner — one of those pawns is an illusion of safety.
hint: Take the **e-file** to the back rank — the corner pawns lock the king instead of helping him.
explanation:
## The Idea

A pure **back-rank mate** with a touch of redundancy: the second rook on **a1** isn't strictly needed, but it removes any doubt that the back rank stays sealed if Black tries something clever.

Play {{Re8#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{g8}}, {{f8}} and the rest of the 8th rank — all controlled by the rook on e8
- {{g7}} and {{h7}} — both blocked by Black's own pawns
- The king cannot capture: e8 is too far from h8
- Black has no pieces left to interpose between e8 and h8

> **Takeaway:** When you have two rooks and the enemy back rank is bare, pick *any* file that lands on the rank — the rook on the safer file usually wins. Always double-check escape squares before committing.
